note: Articles — the curated reading/'what's new' section (the repurposed 'Explore'). Two kinds: 'authored' (has a `body` of blocks, read on-site) and 'link' (curated external piece, `sourceUrl`, opens out). Sort by `date` (newest first) drives the freshness/'recently added' behaviour. Show the AI-draft notice unless `reviewStatus: 'human-reviewed'`. The two entries below are SAMPLES to demonstrate the templates — replace them.

topics.label: Practice & pedagogy
topics.description: Using AI well in real teaching — the craft, not the hype.

topics.label: Safety & ethics
topics.description: Child safety, data, equity, and where AI does not belong.

topics.label: Tools & what's new
topics.description: New tools, changed approvals, things worth knowing this term.

topics.label: Perspectives
topics.description: Thought-provoking takes on AI in education, ours and others'.

articles.title: Sample article — replace me
articles.dek: This is a placeholder authored piece. It exists only to show how an on-site article renders; swap it for a real one.
articles.author: Your name here

body.content: This is a sample article body. Authored pieces are written directly in articles.json as an ordered list of content blocks, the same way track sections work — so the reading experience stays consistent across the site.
You can use text, headings, lists, quotes and callouts. Keep paragraphs short and scannable — teachers are reading between other things.
A good article leaves the reader a little more thoughtful about their practice than when they arrived.
Replace this sample with a real piece when you're ready. Until an article is marked reviewStatus: 'human-reviewed', it carries the AI-draft notice.

articles.title: Sample curated link — replace me
articles.dek: This is a placeholder for a curated external piece. On the list it opens straight out to the source; use it to point teachers at reading worth their time elsewhere.
